name: wildfire_power_shutdown_oxygen_dependence
premise: County emergency director Aaron Bell must decide whether to authorize a preemptive power shutdown as a wind-driven wildfire approaches transmission lines feeding three mountain towns. Utility engineers say leaving the lines energized could spark multiple new fires and trap thousands more residents. Home-health agencies warn that nearly four hundred people in the shutdown zone rely on electric oxygen concentrators, refrigerated insulin, or powered feeding equipment, and many cannot evacuate quickly because the main canyon road is already partially blocked. The county has only sixty portable generators available and a spotty registry of medically fragile residents. If Aaron delays the shutdown to support home medical equipment, a line ignition under forecasted wind conditions could create a regional firestorm within hours.

agents.name: Aaron Bell
agents.identity: I am the county emergency director responsible for making life-and-death decisions before the smoke proves whether they were right. I know mass-casualty wildfire prevention and individual medical dependence are colliding in the same clock.
agents.goal: I want to reduce overall loss of life without abandoning residents whose survival depends on electricity tonight. I need to decide whether broader fire prevention or immediate medical continuity should govern the shutdown decision.

agents.name: Nurse Carla Jimenez
agents.identity: I am a home-health nurse who knows many of the medically fragile residents by name, not by spreadsheet. When officials talk about shutoff zones and incident models, I think about patients who simply stop breathing when the power does.
agents.goal: I want Aaron to delay or narrow the shutdown long enough to relocate the most vulnerable patients. I believe a fire-prevention measure becomes morally compromised if it knowingly strands people on life-sustaining equipment without realistic backup.

choices: I will authorize the full preemptive shutdown now to reduce the risk of new wildfire ignitions, even though some medically fragile residents may lose critical power.
I will delay or limit the shutdown to preserve power for medically dependent residents, accepting a greater chance that energized lines could spark additional fires.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Aaron Bell must decide whether to cut power before the wildfire reaches the transmission corridor. What does Aaron Bell do?
